signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Founded in 1945, Partizan started to make noise in European competitions when Dragan Kicanovic and Drazen Dalipagic led the club to Korac Cup glory in 1978 and 1979. Vlade Divac and Zarko Paspalj helped Partizan reach the 1988 EuroLeague Final Four and lift the 1989 Korac Cup. In 1992, Partizan won the club's first – and, to date, only – EuroLeague crown. With Zeljko Obradovic on the bench, it downed Joventut Badaona 70-71 on a miraculous three-pointer by Sasha Djordjevic. Partizan returned to the EuroLeague Final Four in 1998, but it lost in the semifinals against eventual champion Kinder Bologna. The club continued developing players while dominating domestically, and with a fan base that treats basketball like a religion, Partizan kept growing. In 2010, in its third consecutive EuroLeague playoff appearance, Partizan ousted Maccabi Tel Aviv to reach the Final Four, where it lost against Olympiacos Piraeus after overtime in the semis. A slow decline ensued, resulting in a rare title dry spell. The return of Obradovic to the bench in the summer of 2021 changed things, turning Partizan games into must-see events. Partizan reached the EuroLeague Playoffs and won the ABA League in 2023 for its first major trophy in a decade. In each of the past two seasons, Coach Obradovic's men narrowly missed the playoffs, but won ABA League and Serbian championships, as Partizan remains on a mission to recapture its former glory.

heroMedia.title: Partizan inks talented Uros Mijailovic to a long-term deal
heroMedia.summary: Mijailovic has five EuroLeague appearances this season

content.text:
Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade invested in its future announcing on Monday a multiyear deal with guard [Uros Mijailovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/uros-mijailovic/012943/), who just 18 years of age 10 days ago. This is the first professional contract with Mijailovic who has played for Partizan youth categories, and is expected to play for the club’s U18 team at the upcoming adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifier in Belgrade.

Mijailovic made his professional debut for Partizan last season in the Serbian League playoffs, when he helped the team win the championship scoring 8.3 points.

This season, he has made five brief appearances in the EuroLeague, but is yet to score a point. In the Adriatic League, Mijailovic has played seven games averaging 15 minutes and posting 4.3 points and 1.6 rebounds.

Nakon punoletstva, Uroš Mijailović je potpisao profesionalni ugovor sa crno-belima!🖤🤍

Momak iz Mionice, rođen 2008. godine, obavezao se na višegodišnju vernost Partizanu. pic.twitter.com/VopgY8kogf

— KK Partizan Mozzart Bet (@PartizanBC) March 16, 2026

articleMetadata.title: Partizan inks talented Uros Mijailovic to a long-term deal

This is the first professional contract with Mijailovic who has played for Partizan youth categories, and is expected to play for the club’s U18 team at the upcoming adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifier in Belgrade.

heroMedia.title: Isaac Bonga’s career night leads Partizan past Virtus
heroMedia.summary: A career-best PIR from Isaac Bonga and clutch scoring from Carlik Jones fuel Partizan’s statement win in Bologna

linkedIds.text: Virtus Bologna v Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (2026-03-11T19:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/par/) has been playing better in recent games and it seemed only a matter of time before registering a significant road victory. After a home win against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens and narrow losses - 4 points or less - against Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv, Real Madrid and Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul, Partizan topped [Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) 82-88 in Round 31 and showed a lot of good along the way. 

No-one offered more energy and was more effective than versatile forward [Isaac Bonga](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/isaac-bonga/006760/), who was the best player on the floor. He finished the game with 16 points, 11 rebounds, 5 assists, 2 blocks and 6 fouls drawn for a career-high PIR of 34. It was his fifth career double-double. [Tonye Jekiri](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tonye-jekiri/009733/) took advantage of his extended playing time due to [Bruno Fernando](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/bruno-fernando/013667/)'s foul trouble to post 10 points, 6 rebounds, 2 assists and 1 steal.

"[It was] a physical game, a hustle game, just making sure they don't get energy from the little things like offensive rebounds, 50-50 balls, and I think in the second half, we just did a better job," Bonga said. "I think we just adjusted. We adjusted in the second half, played definitely way more calmly, made sure that we secured the ball, and the good thing is that we secured the win in the end."

content.title: Virtus Bologna-Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade | Round 31 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
There was more good news for coach [Joan Penarroya](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/par/penarroya-joan)'s team. [Carlik Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/carlik-jones/013369/) confirmed his good form after missing 23 regular season games due to injury. He had 19 points in 26 minutes and stepped up big down the stretch. With the game on the line, Jones buried a turnaround jumper and added a backdoor layup off a Virtus timeout to seal the outcome, 74-84, in the next-to-last minute. 

"I'm blessed to be back. I'm so glad to be back. I miss them [Partizan fans] as much as they miss me, having all these fans traveling. We have always been blessed to have that kind of thing. And they helped us tonight," Jones said after the game, pointing out his team's competitiveness. 

"We are here to compete still. A lot of things that's going on, no matter what, we are still going to play hard. We are still going to try to defend our home court, and try to win on the road as well. And we are going to play this thing out."

BRAVOOO CRNO-BELI!🖤🤍#KKPartizan #EuroLeague pic.twitter.com/h5SgmTsghv

— KK Partizan Mozzart Bet (@PartizanBC) March 11, 2026

Partizan played the role of spoiler. Despite not having significant postseason chances, it beat a team that is ahead of it in the standings. Virtus took a step backwards in its goal to reach the Play-In Showdown. After the game, Coach Penarroya praised his team for its defense and hustle.

"In the last month, in the last five weeks, we competed very well. I think we deserve to win the game tonight, and I think we deserved to win other games in other weeks. We played better in the last quarter, and especially we controlled our defense better in the second half," Coach Penarroya said.

"We played better defense, especially in the second half, we controlled their transition play. We improved in defensive rebounds. Only our turnovers were a little bit of a problem, but I think we controlled the pace and we deserved to win."  

Partizan plays six of its eight remaining games in Belgrade, five at home and one at archrival Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade.

linkedId.text: Virtus Bologna v Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (2026-03-11T19:45:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Isaac Bonga’s career night leads Partizan past Virtus
articleMetadata.description: Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade earned a standout 82–88 road win over Virtus Bologna in Round 31, powered by Isaac Bonga’s career-high PIR 34 performance and Carlik Jones’s clutch fourth-quarter scoring. Despite limited postseason prospects, Partizan impressed with improved defense, hustle and composure, handing Virtus a setback in its Play-In pursuit. With six of their final eight games in Belgrade, Partizan looks to build on its strongest stretch of the season.

heroMedia.title: Partizan loses Duane Washington to calf injury
heroMedia.summary: Washington already missed six games this season with a back problem

content.text: [Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/par/) will be without its top scorer [Duane Washington](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/duane-washington/013370/) in the upcoming weeks due to a calf injury. Washington reported pain in his calf during Friday’s practice, Partizan announced. The medical test confirmed an injury that will sideline the scoring guard for the next few weeks.

Duejn Vašington van stroja zbog povrede lista.

Košarkaš Partizana Mozzart Bet Duejn Vašington, doživeo je povredu mišića u gornjem delu lista i biće u narednih nekoliko nedelja van tima.

Nakon što je tokom jučerašnjeg treninga prijavio bol, medicinski tim je uputio igrača na… pic.twitter.com/jFcdKYZrg3

— KK Partizan Mozzart Bet (@PartizanBC) March 7, 2026

Washington leads Partizan in scoring with 14.9 points in 23 appearances this season, mostly off the bench. In his second campaign in the competition, Washington is playing 22 minutes per game, and is tied for 10th with 2.1 three-pointers made per game.

Partizan is all but eliminated from postseason contention, sitting in 17th with a 9-20 record. Its next test will be against Virtus in Bologna this coming Wednesday.

articleMetadata.title: Partizan loses Duane Washington to calf injury
articleMetadata.description: Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade will be without its top scorer Duane Washington in the upcoming weeks due to a calf injury. Washington reported pain in his calf during Friday’s practice, Partizan announced. The medical test confirmed an injury that will sideline the scoring guard for the next few weeks.

heroMedia.title: Maccabi - Hapoel and Partizan - Dubai suspended

content.text:
Euroleague Basketball would like to inform that the following EuroLeague Regular Season Round 30 games have been suspended:

Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv - Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv, scheduled to be played on Thursday, March 5.

Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade – Dubai Basketball, scheduled to be played on Thursday, March 5.

The games have been suspended due to the current situation in the region and the resulting inability to travel following the temporary closure of the airspace. Euroleague Basketball will evaluate with the affected teams the best possible options to reschedule the games, while closely monitoring the latest developments and maintaining constant communication with local and international authorities, and all relevant organizations to ensure the safety and well-being of everyone involved. Further information will be released in the coming days.

articleMetadata.title: Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv - Paris Basket suspended
articleMetadata.description:
The EuroLeague Regular Season Round 21 game between Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv and Paris Basket that was scheduled to be played on Tuesday, March 3 in Tel Aviv has been suspended due to the current situation in the region.

Euroleague Basketball will evaluate with the affected teams the best possible options to reschedule the game. Further information will be released in the coming days.

heroMedia.title: Jabari Parker departs Partizan on loan
heroMedia.summary: He has joined Spanish side Joventut Badalona

content.text:
[Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/par/) has confirmed that [Jabari Parker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jabari-parker/012745/) has left the club to join Spanish League team Joventut Badalona on loan for the remainder of the 2025-26 season.

Parker had fallen out of favor at Partizan and last played in Round 21. In 15 EuroLeague appearances since signing for the Belgrade-based outfit last summer, he averaged 7.7 points and 2.5 rebounds.

This was the 30-year-old's third season in the EuroLeague, having spent the previous two campaigns with FC Barcelona.

Džabari Parker na pozajmici u Huventudu!

Džabari Parker će u nastavku tekuće sezone igrati za Huventud iz Badalone. Tim iz Španije će do kraja sezone preuzeti deo plate za igrača koji je početkom sezone potpisao dvogodišnji ugovor sa Partizanom Mozzart Bet.

U prethodnim… pic.twitter.com/eZprzfeaJJ

— KK Partizan Mozzart Bet (@PartizanBC) February 26, 2026

"Jabari Parker will play the remainder of the current season for Joventut Badalona," Partizan announced in a statement. "The Spanish club will cover part of the salary through the end of the season for the player who signed a two-year contract with Partizan Mozzart Bet at the start of the season.

"In recent weeks, Parker has been training individually in order to reach optimal form, and he will now have the opportunity to prove himself on the court with Joventut."

articleMetadata.title: Jabari Parker departs Partizan on loan
articleMetadata.description: Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade has confirmed that Jabari Parker has left the club to join Spanish League team Joventut Badalona on loan for the remainder of the 2025-26 season.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

heroMedia.title: Talen Horton-Tucker shines as Fenerbahce edges Partizan
heroMedia.summary: The guard poured in a career-high 29 points to steer the champs past the Serbian side

linkedIds.text: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul v Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (2026-02-25T17:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/ulk/) remained in the EuroLeague’s driving seat and improved its record to 21-7 as [Talen Horton-Tucker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/talen-horton-tucker/014124/) sank a personal-best 29 points with the hosts celebrating an eighth successive win after a titanic tussle at Ulker Sports Hall.

Horton-Tucker shot an impressive 10-of-12 from the floor as Partizan led more or less throughout the opening three quarters before Fenerbahce beefed up its defense and nosed ahead for good early in the fourth.

A 9-2 run gave the home team a 66-60 advantage and Horton-Tucker then hit a crunch three to fend off a Partizan comeback, although the visitors kept breathing down the champion’s neck until the bitter end.

Cool free-throw shooting from [Tarik Biberovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tarik-biberovic/006961/), who sank the last 4 points for his team from the foul line, kept Partizan at bay as [Carlik Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/carlik-jones/013369/) – playing for the first time since Round 5 after recovering from injury – came up well short from downtown on the buzzer in a desperate attempt to force overtime.

content.title: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul-Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Fenerbahce head coach Saras Jasikevicius was pleased with the outcome, although he conceded that his men played in fits and starts, with Partizan making life difficult for the EuroLeague leader as big man [Bruno Fernando](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/bruno-fernando/013667/) caused all sorts of problems in the paint. 

“Our team game was not really functioning in some situations, so we just had to put our heads down and find the right matchups,” Jasikevicius said. “In the second half, we made much less mental mistakes and turnovers.”

[Wade Baldwin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/wade-baldwin-iv/009863/) chipped in with 17 points for Fenerbahce, Biberovic netted all 13 of his points in the second half and [Bonzie Colson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/bonzie-colson/009783/) added 10 as the hosts overcame a poor shooting night. The Turkish side made only 6-of-24 from deep and committed 11 turnovers against just 12 assists, but it picked up 15 offensive boards while containing Partizan to just 6.

Aggressive defending down the stretch and Horton-Tucker’s heroics ultimately made the difference as Fenerbahce held Partizan center Fernando to just 2 points in the second half for a total of 18. Jones added 17 after returning to action for the first time since October and [Duane Washington](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/duane-washington/013370/) netted 15 in defeat.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Fenerbahçe Beko (@fbbasketbol)

“I just wanted to come out and be aggressive,” Horton-Tucker said in his postgame comments. “We have a lot of injured players right now and tonight’s performance says a lot about our group. Having guys like this makes it easy to keep pushing and fighting. I am loving every second here and I hope we can keep winning,” he added.

Colson revealed that Fenerbahce’s players had to take some stick from Jasikevicius during the interval.

“In the first half, we weren’t as aggressive as we should be, but we regrouped at halftime after the coach got on us a little bit and we are happy to take the win,” he said. “The rebounds got us going offensively and we were effective when we needed to be.”

It was another bitter pill to swallow for Partizan head coach Joan Penarroya, as the Spaniard saw his team suffer yet another tough loss after being on top for much of the contest and drop to 9-20. Partizan seems to lack the cutting edge when the chips are down and Penarroya acknowledged there was work to do to get on the right side of those fine margins.

“Small details again and we’ve now lost four games in the last minute,” he said. “We competed well, so congratulations to both my players and Fenerbahce. We’ve just got to control the small details better.”

articleMetadata.title: Talen Horton-Tucker shines as Fenerbahce edges Partizan
articleMetadata.description: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul remained in the EuroLeague’s driving seat and improved its record to 21-7 as Talen Horton-Tucker sank a personal-best 29 points with the hosts celebrating an eighth successive win after a titanic tussle at Ulker Sports Hall

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).
